code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test03-FEB-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria or leukocyturia. These findings may indicate renal tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
